mappings.rationaleAI: Photosynthesis directly covers the light-dependent reactions, the Calvin cycle (carbon fixation), chlorophyll and photosynthetic pigments, photophosphorylation, C3/C4/CAM pathways, and the efficiency of photosynthesis -- the entire photosynthesis subsection.

Mitochondria's role in cellular respiration and ATP synthesis through oxidative phosphorylation directly addresses the combustion of food materials and energy conservation through cellular respiration.
Enzyme regulation through allosteric control and feedback inhibition directly relates to the regulation of metabolism through enzymatic control mechanisms.
Macromolecules including proteins, nucleic acids, carbohydrates, and lipids represent the products of biosynthesis (anabolism) -- the building of cell components from simpler precursors.
Free radicals and oxidative processes in cellular damage and defense relate to oxidation reactions within cellular respiration and the metabolic balance of oxidation and transduction.
Phytoplankton as major producers of atmospheric oxygen through photosynthesis illustrates the biological importance of photosynthesis at the global scale.
The carbon cycle and carbon's role in organic chemistry connect to the metabolic path of carbon in photosynthesis through the carbon reduction cycle.
